key_departures.impact: Durant was the Rams' primary CB1 over the past two seasons, starting 32 games across 2024-25 and recording 3 INTs and 7 PDs in 2025. His departure leaves a slot/boundary corner hole, but is directly offset by the McDuffie trade and Watson signing. The McDuffie/Watson acquisitions made Durant expendable.
Williams announced his retirement and was placed on the Reserve/Retired list. Was a cap casualty candidate regardless. Saves ~$7.5M. Depth loss but not a starter-level departure.

Havenstein retired after 11 seasons as the Rams' starting right tackle. Warren McClendon Jr. is expected to step into the starting RT role and graded out as PFF's 17th-ranked offensive tackle in 2025 while filling in. Creates a depth/developmental need at OT but McClendon is viewed as a capable successor.

Garoppolo served as Matthew Stafford's backup for two seasons but barely played. His departure (or potential return on a minimum deal) creates a need for a backup QB. Cardinals interest reported. Rams have not yet replaced him.
Witherspoon missed most of 2025 with multiple clavicle injuries. Depth departure; given injury history the Rams are expected to move on. CB room fully restructured around McDuffie and Watson.
Mid-season trade deadline acquisition who played in the playoffs. Best fit in the slot. With McDuffie and Watson now in the fold, his return to the Rams seems unlikely. Moderate depth departure.
Atwell was paid ~$10M APY in 2025 but featured in only 16.5% of snaps. Expected to depart. Creates mild need for a WR4/5 speedster but not a starter-level hole.
McQuaide was an emergency mid-season signing in 2025. His departure was expected; replaced directly by Joe Cardona signing.

signings.analysis: The centerpiece move of the Rams' offseason. Two-time All-Pro, first-team All-Pro in 2023, second-team All-Pro in 2024. Contract ($31M AAV, $100M guaranteed) makes him the highest-paid CB in NFL history, surpassing Sauce Gardner. This is a long-term franchise cornerstone signing. CB goes from the team's most glaring need to a strength with McDuffie plus Watson. The pick cost (No. 29, two 2026 picks, 2027 3rd) is steep but reflects the Rams' all-in posture with Matthew Stafford. Rams retain the No. 13 overall pick.
signings.notes: Trade agreed March 5; extension agreed March 8 per NFL.com/ESPN. Official at start of new league year March 11.

signings.analysis: Watson is coming off the best season of his career, finishing with career-highs in defensive snaps (96%), interceptions (2), and tackles (64). Reunited with former KC teammate Trent McDuffie. Three-year deal at $17M AAV with $34M guaranteed is a genuine starter investment, not a bridge deal. Paired with McDuffie, the Rams have completely rebuilt a cornerback room that was a Super Bowl-level liability. CB need is fully addressed for 2026 and beyond.
signings.notes: Reported by Ian Rapoport/Tom Pelissero/Mike Garafolo; official March 11.

signings.analysis: Curl was the Rams' second-leading tackler in 2025 (122 total tackles, 2nd-most among all NFL DBs), posted a career-best 77.8 PFF grade, and made the decisive playoff interception against the Bears in OT. Re-signing him at $12M AAV with $24M guaranteed locks in one half of a strong safety tandem (with Quentin Lake, who signed a 3-year extension in January 2026). S need is fully addressed. Max value of $39M further incentivizes performance.
signings.notes: Reported by Ian Rapoport/Tom Pelissero/Mike Garafolo on March 6; confirmed by multiple outlets.

signings.analysis: Re-signing Higbee keeps all four key TEs (Higbee, Colby Parkinson, Davis Allen, Terrance Ferguson) under contract, preserving the Rams' 13-personnel identity that led the NFL in total offense. At $4M AAV with a max of $8M and age-33/injury concerns, this is a low-cost veteran retention, not a long-term anchor. Higbee missed 7 games in 2025 with an ankle injury. TE depth is addressed but the position still warrants monitoring given injury history. Need partially addressed.
signings.notes: Agreed March 5, per therams.com. Agent Erik Burkhardt confirmed. Max $8M per ESPN's Adam Schefter.

signings.analysis: Thomas returns for a fifth season as a rotational edge rusher. One-year deal signals depth/rotational role behind Jared Verse and Byron Young. Terms not disclosed. Low-impact signing that maintains roster continuity at EDGE depth.

11-year veteran long snapper (10 seasons with Patriots, 1 with Dolphins) directly addresses the Rams' special teams instability from 2025. Cardona overlapped with new Rams special teams coordinator Bubba Ventrone at New England (2015-17). Two-year deal provides stability. Replaces the stop-gap McQuaide. ST need fully addressed.
signings.notes: Reported March 10 by Orange County Register/multiple outlets. Confirmed by therams.com.

signings.analysis: Primarily a special teams player in 2025, seeing only 12 offensive snaps all season. Re-signs for approximately league minimum. Provides OT depth/swing tackle competition behind Alaric Jackson and Warren McClendon Jr. Does not address the post-Havenstein RT depth need in any meaningful starting capacity. OT depth need remains.
signings.notes: Re-signed February 18 per therams.com announcement. Spotrac contract data confirmed.

signings.analysis: Tendered as an exclusive rights free agent (ERFA) at league minimum, locking him in for 2026. Mevis was outstanding after being signed mid-season, going 18-of-19 on FGs and 48-of-48 on XPs in 2025. Hit the game-winning OT FG vs. Bears in the divisional round. K need nonexistent — Mevis locked in for essentially nothing.
signings.notes: Tendered along with Justin Dedich and Xavier Smith. ERFA, cannot negotiate elsewhere.

signings.analysis: Interior OL depth retained at minimum via ERFA tender. Dedich can play both guard spots and started six games in 2025. Low-cost depth retention that does not address any starter-level IOL need but solidifies the back end of the interior line group.
signings.notes: Tendered as ERFA along with Mevis and Xavier Smith.

signings.analysis: Primary punt returner and receiver depth retained at minimum. Posted career-high 303 receiving yards in 2025. With Tutu Atwell's likely departure, Smith could see expanded WR role. ERFA tender is low-risk roster management. Does not address the WR depth need created by Atwell's departure at a meaningful level.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: McDuffie (4yr/$124M, $100M gtd, record AAV of $31M) + Watson (3yr/$51M, $34M gtd) = complete and generational overhaul of what was the team's single biggest weakness. McDuffie is the highest-paid CB in NFL history, a 2x All-Pro who starts immediately. Watson is a legit CB2 investment, not depth. CB went from a team-defining liability (gave up 346 yards/3 TDs to Darnold in the NFCCG) to a position of strength. Still retain Emmanuel Forbes Jr. CB need is fully eliminated. Only potential concern is long-term roster depth behind these two — a Day 3 camp body is all that's needed.

Curl re-signed (3yr/$36M, $24M gtd) locked in alongside Quentin Lake (3-year extension signed January 2026). Both starters under contract for multiple years. Safety is now a strength, not a need. No draft capital required at the position.

Rob Havenstein's retirement creates a starting RT vacancy that Warren McClendon Jr. is expected to fill (graded PFF 17th among OTs in 2025 while filling in). However, Havenstein's loss is real — he was a 11-year starter. Quessenberry's re-signing provides zero meaningful starting depth. The Rams need to address OT in the draft (Day 1 or 2) or via later free agency. Left tackle Alaric Jackson is set, but the RT depth chart is thin. Escalated from pre-draft need to a draft-day priority.

Jimmy Garoppolo departed as a UFA and has not been replaced. The Rams need a backup QB capable of spot-starting behind the 38-year-old Matthew Stafford. Cardinals interest in Garoppolo has been reported; the Rams may attempt to re-sign him or pursue alternatives. This is a priority need that has been escalated by the absence of any QB signing to date. Matthew Stafford remains the unquestioned starter but the backup situation is unresolved.

Higbee re-signed (2yr/$8M max) preserves the full 4-TE group (Higbee, Parkinson, Allen, Ferguson) that powered the Rams' league-leading offense in 13-personnel. However, Higbee is 33 and injury-prone (7 missed games in 2025). This is a low-cost, short-term re-sign; Parkinson is the functional TE1. Need is partially managed but a developmental TE or a younger TE1 upgrade is still worth pursuing in mid-to-late draft rounds. Tier stays the same.

Tutu Atwell's likely departure ($10M APY in 2025 but barely used) leaves a WR4/5 vacancy. Xavier Smith retained via ERFA at minimum provides some depth. With Puka Nacua and Davante Adams in place, WR1/WR2 are set. But Adams is 33 and on a $28M cap hit, and there is no WR3 of consequence. The Rams are widely projected to target a WR at No. 13 in the draft (USC's Makai Lemon frequently mocked). Escalated slightly due to Atwell loss and long-term Adams concerns.

No FA activity at LB. Nate Landman is signed on an extension, but the Rams did not address MLB in free agency despite Omar Speights being viewed as a weak link at MLB. No top-101 LB was available per reports. LB remains a genuine need — particularly at MLB — that the Rams will need to address in the draft. Tier unchanged.
No IDL movement in free agency. Kobie Turner and Braden Fiske anchor the interior. No changes to this need tier.

key_additions.headline: Record-setting blockbuster trade lands 2x All-Pro, NFL's highest-paid CB to fix the Rams' most glaring weakness
Reunited with McDuffie from KC, Watson provides legit CB2 investment to complete full secondary overhaul
Career-best 2025 (122 tackles, playoff-deciding INT) earns Curl market-rate re-sign, locking in top safety tandem with Lake
Franchise TE legend returns on low-cost deal, preserving all four TEs that powered league-leading 13-personnel offense
11-year veteran long snapper stabilizes chaotic special teams unit; reunited with new ST coordinator Bubba Ventrone

key_losses.headline: 11-year franchise RT retires, leaving Warren McClendon Jr. as heir apparent and OT depth as a draft priority
Starting CB departs for Dallas on one-year deal; his void made immaterial by the massive McDuffie/Watson CB overhaul
Veteran CB and fan favorite retires; saves ~$7.5M in cap space as Rams fully rebuild cornerback room
Backup QB departs as UFA, leaving Stafford without a proven replacement and creating an unresolved need behind center

notes: Contract guarantees for Higbee, Keir Thomas, and Cardona are partially confirmed (Higbee max $8M per ESPN; Cardona terms not fully disclosed; Thomas terms not disclosed). All other core contract data sourced from NFL.com, ESPN, Spotrac, and Ian Rapoport/Tom Pelissero/Mike Garafolo reports. McDuffie extension ($100M guaranteed) confirmed by ESPN's Adam Schefter. Curl guarantees ($24M) confirmed by AP/CBS. Watson guarantees ($34M) confirmed by NFL Network Insiders. Cap space figures from OverTheCap as of March 13, 2026. ERFA tender contract values are league minimum (exact dollar amounts not reported). Rams traded away 2026 picks Nos. 29 (1st), a 5th, and a 6th in the McDuffie deal, retaining their No. 13 overall pick (acquired from Atlanta). Draft capital impact is significant.
